notes: Roman poet known for his Odes and Art of Poetry; served briefly as military tribune before focusing on literature.

docs.title: Letter LVII
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … it correspond with it." Horace too, an acute and learned writer, in his Art of Poetry gives the same advice to the skilled translator:-- And care not thou with over anxious thought To render word for word. Terence has translated Menander; Plautus and CÃ¦cilius the old comic poets.

docs.author: Suetonius

docs.author: Suetonius
